description: Manually seeded if-then reasoning units distilled from RCA analysis traces. Each unit's `trigger` (the IF) is an event-pattern predicate; its `reasoning` (the THEN) is a CoT fragment with <placeholder> variables the Analyst binds from matched events. The composer selects matching units and concatenates them into the analysis procedure for a new case.

units.name: Anomaly overview and candidate list
units.reasoning:
Step: Build the candidate list.
I list the strongest metric_anomaly events as <metric_anomaly_events>, grouped by service and signal, preferring persistent runs with large |z|.
I keep only services in <candidate_components> (possible_root_cause_components) as root-cause candidates; other services are supporting or propagation nodes.
I note, for each candidate, which local signal (cpu, mem, diskio, socket, latency) deviates and how persistently.

units.name: CPU saturation localization
units.reasoning:
Step: CPU check.
I list cpu metric_anomaly events as <cpu_metric_events>.
If one candidate service shows a persistent upward cpu deviation across consecutive windows while <other_service_metric_events> stay near baseline, that service_cpu is a strong root-cause candidate.
CPU faults often co-raise latency downstream; a co-located cpu anomaly outranks the latency it causes.

units.name: Memory growth localization
units.reasoning:
Step: Memory check.
I list upward mem metric_anomaly events as <mem_metric_events>.
A sustained upward mem deviation (step change or monotonic growth) on a candidate service points to service_mem.
I ignore downward mem deviations: they are GC or load drops, not a memory fault.

units.name: Disk IO localization
units.reasoning:
Step: Disk IO check.
I list upward diskio metric_anomaly events as <diskio_metric_events>.
Disk activity is near-zero in baseline for most services, so a large sustained diskio deviation on a candidate is a decisive service_diskio signal, even if cpu or latency also move.

units.name: Socket exhaustion localization
units.reasoning:
Step: Socket check.
I list upward socket metric_anomaly events as <socket_metric_events>.
Socket counts are small in baseline, so persistence matters more than magnitude: a persistent socket rise on a candidate indicates service_socket.
Socket exhaustion frequently co-raises cpu on the SAME service (<same_service_cpu_events>); when both rise together on one candidate, I prefer socket as the reason because it is the injected resource, with cpu as its effect.

units.name: Latency: root cause vs propagation
units.reasoning:
Step: Latency locality.
I separate local latency metric_anomaly events <latency_metric_events> (kpi latency.p50/latency.p90) from edge span_slowdown events <span_slowdown_events> (kpi latency.p99).
span_slowdown uses RAW span duration, which includes downstream wait, so a slow CALLER edge is expected propagation and is NOT evidence that the caller is the root cause.
I localize a latency root cause (service_latency, e.g. a delay/loss fault) to the candidate whose OWN latency.p50/p90 METRIC z is the highest, provided it has no co-located cpu/mem/diskio/socket anomaly; trace slowdown into that service only confirms it.
I do not move rank1 to a caller just because its edge p99 is large; if a service has a co-located resource anomaly, I treat its latency as a symptom of that resource fault.

units.name: Error-code cascade to deepest callee
units.reasoning:
Step: Error cascade.
I list error_code events as <error_code_events> and follow them along <call_edge_events>.
Errors cascade upward from the failing dependency, so I attribute them to the deepest or earliest callee emitting non-OK status, not to the callers that merely propagate the failure.
Errors validate impact and timing but do not by themselves set the reason; I still take the reason from component-local metric evidence.

units.name: Log confirmation of the leading candidate
units.reasoning:
Step: Log confirmation.
I inspect log_pattern events for the leading candidate as <same_service_log_events> and for neighbors as <neighbor_log_events>, using compact fields only (id, template_id, level, z, new_template).
Error/warning templates or new templates on the candidate, or a throughput drop (negative count z) consistent with the anomaly window, confirm timing and impact.
Logs confirm or add supporting evidence; they never override strong component-local metric evidence, and I never copy raw template text into findings.

units.name: Final reason reranker
units.reasoning:
Step: Final rerank.
I compare accumulated candidates <accumulated_candidate_rankings> and keep the component with the strongest, most persistent component-local metric evidence unless a rival candidate has stronger local evidence.
For the selected component I compare its cpu, mem, diskio, socket, and latency evidence <selected_component_metric_evidence> and set the reason from the strongest LOCAL signal; a diskio or socket signal that is near-zero in baseline outranks a co-moving cpu or latency.
I downgrade latency when it is supported mainly by trace impact, downstream latency, or neighbor symptoms rather than local metric evidence.
I output the top-3 as component/reason pairs drawn only from the candidate lists, each with a short justification citing event ids.
